text.title: OST Node Taxonomy and Tree Rules
text.summary: Defines the four OST node types, their constraints, evidence requirements, and the opportunity-vs-solution discipline that prevents the most common failure mode.

rule.statement: Every tree has exactly one Outcome node at the root — a measurable product or business goal with an explicit metric.
rule.rationale: Multiple outcomes in one tree create a hydra: teams cannot prune branches when priorities conflict.

rule.statement: Opportunity nodes must be phrased in customer language describing a problem or unmet need, not a feature or solution.
rule.rationale: Solution-phrased opportunities ("add export button") collapse the discovery process; they skip validation of whether the need exists.

rule.statement: Every Opportunity node must carry at least two evidence references (interview IDs, support ticket IDs, or analytics segment IDs) or be marked unvalidated and scheduled for pruning.
rule.rationale: Evidence references are the only signal that distinguishes a real customer need from a brainstorm item.

rule.statement: Solution nodes are mutually exclusive within an opportunity: pick one to test, not all simultaneously.
rule.rationale: Testing multiple solutions at once prevents attribution of results to a specific solution.

rule.statement: Experiment nodes require a written kill criterion (hypothesis, method, success metric, sample size, status). Without a kill criterion, experiments never end.
rule.rationale: Open-ended experiments consume resources indefinitely and produce ambiguous results.

rule.statement: Keep tree depth at most 4 levels: Outcome → Opportunity → Solution → Experiment.
rule.rationale: Deeper trees indicate scope confusion; the extra levels are almost always sub-opportunities or sub-experiments that should be sibling nodes.

rule.statement: A node is an Opportunity if it describes a customer problem in customer language. A node is a Solution if it describes a product response. Run a node-classifier lint before adding any node.
rule.rationale: Teams under delivery pressure reclassify solutions as opportunities to skip validation; a classifier subagent rejects or rewrites ambiguous nodes.

antipattern.reason: This is a solution, not a customer need. Rewrite as: "Users lose data when browser crashes before they can download."
Too vague to have evidence; cite specific interview IDs or rewrite with a concrete scenario.

section.title: Tree as structured data

rule.statement: Maintain the OST as YAML or JSON (not only as a Miro/FigJam diagram) so agents can edit the data and humans review the visual rendering.
rule.rationale: Agents flatten parent-child semantics when editing a diagram directly; the YAML/JSON is the canonical source of truth.

section.reference: Agent-editable OST schema with outcome, opportunities, solutions, and experiments
